coaching.coaching: Plan how back-compat re-export and size acceptance criteria will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: back-compat re-export and size acceptance criteria

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: verify command `! grep -q 'lint_refs' docs/architecture.md` greps the unanchored word 'lint_refs' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_lint_refs'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"lint_refs":')
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Anchor AC5's grep to the remedy row's actual content (e.g., `! grep -q 'lint_refs\.py' docs/architecture.md`) or add a clarifying note that ALL occurrences of 'lint_refs' in arch.md must be removed — not just the remedy row.
evidence: Plan states: 'This ticket deletes that stale/superseded arch.md row' — intent is to remove one row, but the grep checks for ANY occurrence of the substring 'lint_refs' anywhere in arch.md.
If arch.md mentions lint_refs in a comment, table-of-contents, or cross-reference section, the AC would require removing those too, silently expanding scope beyond what the plan describes.
findings.finding: AC5's grep pattern `! grep -q 'lint_refs' docs/architecture.md` is an unanchored substring search that would fail (requiring deletion of ALL occurrences of 'lint_refs' in arch.md) if the file contains any other mention of 'lint_refs' beyond the remedy row — e.g., a cross-reference, historical note, or comment — making the proving command a potential false-positive per defect taxonomy item (1).
scenarios: arch.md contains a cross-reference or comment mentioning lint_refs outside the remedy row; executor deletes only the remedy row; AC5's grep still fails; executor must hunt down additional occurrences not described in the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is solely to verify the remedy row is gone, replace with a more targeted command such as `! grep -q 'lint_refs\.py' docs/architecture.md` (anchoring on the filename pattern), or document that any other arch.md references to lint_refs must also be cleaned up as part of this ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten AC5 (or add prose) so it verifies deletion of the specific offenders-table row for lint_refs.py rather than a blanket absence of the string 'lint_refs' from all of arch.md — e.g., grep for the table row pattern or quote the exact line to be removed.
evidence: Deviation section: 'This ticket deletes that stale/superseded arch.md row (arch.md tracks active oversized modules; a resolved module's row is removed, not rewritten).'
findings.finding: AC5 uses a broad grep (`! grep -q 'lint_refs' docs/architecture.md`) that verifies 'lint_refs' is absent from ALL of arch.md, but the Deviation section only describes deleting one specific row. If 'lint_refs' appears anywhere else in arch.md (e.g., a cross-reference, a module inventory table, or a historical note), AC5 will demand removal of those references too — work not described in the plan body — or conversely will pass even if the target row is replaced with equivalent text.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 / Deviation from docs/architecture.md section
scenarios: arch.md has a module inventory or cross-reference table that also lists lint_refs.py — AC5 fails even though the remedy row is properly deleted, blocking merge on out-of-scope work.
Implementer removes only the remedy row but AC5 still fails because lint_refs is mentioned in a comment, revealing that the AC is stricter than the described work.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC5 with a grep targeting the specific row pattern: `! grep -q 'lint_refs\.py.*offender\|remedy.*lint_refs' docs/architecture.md` or document in the plan body which other occurrences (if any) of 'lint_refs' in arch.md are also in scope for removal.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out note (even one sentence) describing how to restore lint_refs.py to the allowlist/ceilings files if a post-merge regression pushes its LOC back over 800, satisfying T4's requirement for a documented rollback/back-out path.
evidence: Summary: 'Graduate lint_refs.py off the allowlist' — no rollback path stated
findings.finding: The plan removes lint_refs.py from the CI allowlist and ceilings files — a gate change that is not trivially reversible without a follow-up PR — but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan if post-merge CI regressions reveal that the module grows past 800 LOC (e.g., a concurrent PR adds LOC to lint_refs).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 / AC4; Summary section
scenarios: A concurrent PR adds LOC to lint_refs.py; after merge, the module exceeds 800 LOC and CI blocks all subsequent PRs; there is no documented procedure to temporarily re-add lint_refs to the allowlist while the overage is fixed.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'If lint_refs.py grows past 800 LOC post-merge, re-add it to .github/module-size-allowlist.txt with the new ceiling value and open a follow-up refactor ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS` to the explicit re-import list in the Back-compat section (alongside `_OutputContract`, `_Scope`, `_validate_expression`), since it moves to `lint_expr.py` but is imported by `lint.py:27` and `test_contracts.py:19` via `lint_refs`.
evidence: Plan: 'lint_refs re-imports the moved names for its own internal use (_OutputContract, _Scope, _validate_expression) and re-exports ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS (added to __all__)'
Seam section lists ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS as part of the moved cluster
Back-compat section names only three re-imported symbols, not ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS

lint_refs.__all__ (lines 867-870) currently contains only LintFinding and lint_document — ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS is not currently re-exported, so the re-export is new work that must be specified completely
findings.finding: The Back-compat section lists '_OutputContract', '_Scope', '_validate_expression' as the names lint_refs re-imports from lint_expr for internal use, but omits 'ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS' — which also moves to lint_expr.py yet is consumed by lint.py:27 ('from .lint_refs import ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS') and test_contracts.py:19. The plan adds ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS to lint_refs.__all__ but never specifies it as a re-import from lint_expr back into lint_refs. A developer following the Back-compat section verbatim would re-import only the three named symbols and miss the ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS re-export, breaking lint.py and test_contracts.py at import time.
scenarios: Developer follows the Back-compat re-import list literally, re-imports only _OutputContract/_Scope/_validate_expression, omits 'from .lint_expr import ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS' in lint_refs, causing lint.py to fail at import with ImportError.

AC 'Back-compat re-exports + behavior unchanged' pytest suite catches this at test time, but the plan gives no early signal that ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS must be re-imported into lint_refs (not just added to __all__).
findings.suggested_fix: In the Back-compat section, change 'lint_refs re-imports the moved names for its own internal use (_OutputContract, _Scope, _validate_expression)' to 'lint_refs re-imports (_OutputContract, _Scope, _validate_expression, ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS) from lint_expr — the first three for internal use by staying functions; ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS for re-export via __all__ to lint.py:27 and test_contracts.py:19.'

findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

findings.finding: AC3 states that the `docs/architecture.md` remedy row must be deleted, but no proving command confirms this deletion. `make lint` (AC6) is a catch-all that may or may not validate arch.md content, and AC4 (module-size contract test) only verifies the allowlist/ceilings files. An agent could skip the arch.md edit and still pass all stated commands.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — AC3: 'its remedy row deleted from docs/architecture.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: verify with `grep -c 'lint_refs.py' docs/architecture.md` returning 0, or `! grep -q 'lint_refs.py' docs/architecture.md`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
